name: Berlin
localTips: Validate your BVG ticket at the small yellow or red boxes before boarding — inspectors carry no sympathy for 'I forgot' and fines start at €60.
Jaywalking at a red pedestrian light (Ampelmann) is taken seriously here, especially with kids around — locals will tell you off even with no cars in sight.
Many bars and clubs are cash-only or card-only at random, so carry both — and remember tipping is done verbally by rounding up when you pay, not left on the table.
Sunday is Ruhetag: almost all shops are shut, so plan grocery runs for Saturday and treat Sunday as flea-market and park day instead.
State museums on Museum Island are free for under-18s year-round, and many Berlin museums offer a reduced 'last hour' rate — ask at the counter.

activities.title: Brandenburg Gate & Pariser Platz
activities.description: Berlin's defining landmark, floodlit at night and framed by the French and American embassies — walk through the columns where the Wall once cut the square in two.

activities.title: East Side Gallery
activities.description: The longest surviving stretch of the Berlin Wall, painted by artists from around the world in 1990 — Dmitri Vrubel's 'Fraternal Kiss' is the mural everyone stops for.

activities.title: Tiergarten park walk
activities.description: Central Berlin's 200-hectare park, laced with paths from the Siegessäule victory column to the Englischer Garten — locals jog, grill and slackline here all summer.

activities.title: Mauerpark Sunday flea market & karaoke
activities.description: A former death-strip turned Sunday institution — hundreds of flea-market stalls, then crowds gather at the amphitheatre for Bearpit Karaoke as the sun goes down.

activities.title: Memorial to the Murdered Jews of Europe
activities.description: 2,711 grey concrete stelae rising and dipping over undulating ground near Brandenburg Gate — disorienting to walk through, and free to enter at any hour.

activities.title: Ort der Information (free)
activities.description: The underground documentation centre beneath the Holocaust Memorial tells individual victims' stories through letters and diaries — sobering, free, and rarely crowded.

activities.title: Gendarmenmarkt square
activities.description: Berlin's most elegant square, framed by the domed German and French Cathedrals and the Konzerthaus — beautiful any time, magical when the Christmas market fills it.

activities.title: Markthalle Neun covered market
activities.description: An 1891 cast-iron market hall in Kreuzberg — browse fresh produce daily, and join the crowds for Street Food Thursday's global stalls under one roof.

activities.title: Viktoriapark waterfall & views
activities.description: A hilltop park with Berlin's only real waterfall, cascading down toward a war memorial obelisk with skyline views over Kreuzberg — a locals' picnic spot.

activities.title: Museum Island & Lustgarten
activities.description: Walk the Spree island of five world-class museums without paying a cent — the neoclassical facades, the Berliner Dom's dome and the Lustgarten lawns are free to admire.

activities.title: Teufelsberg hilltop & Cold War listening station
activities.description: A rubble hill built from WWII debris in the Grunewald forest, with graffiti-covered NSA radar domes at the summit and sweeping views over the city's western edge.

activities.title: Christmas market at Gendarmenmarkt
activities.description: Berlin's grandest Christmas market fills the square between the two cathedrals with glühwein stands, craft stalls and an ice rink beneath fairy lights.

activities.title: Badeschiff floating pool on the Spree
activities.description: A converted river barge turned open-air swimming pool moored in the Spree, with a sandy beach bar alongside — Berlin's answer to summer heat.

activities.title: Tempelhofer Feld former airfield
activities.description: A decommissioned Nazi-era airport turned vast public park — cycle, kite-surf or picnic on the open runways where planes once landed during the Berlin Airlift.

activities.title: Topography of Terror (free)
activities.description: A permanent outdoor and indoor exhibition on the former Gestapo and SS headquarters site, tracing Nazi terror through original documents along a surviving Wall segment.

activities.title: Beer garden hour at Prater Garten
activities.description: Berlin's oldest beer garden, shaded by chestnut trees in Prenzlauer Berg since 1837 — self-service tables, cheap Bavarian classics and locals sharing benches with strangers.
activities.insiderNote: Bring your own food if you just want a beer — self-service beer gardens in Berlin legally allow it, unlike the attached restaurant section.

activities.title: Klunkerkranich rooftop bar
activities.description: A car-park rooftop in Neukölln turned garden bar with DJs and a view stretching to the TV Tower — the sunset crowd here is almost entirely locals, not tourists.
activities.insiderNote: It's above the Neukölln Arcaden shopping centre — take the lift to the top floor then follow signs up an unmarked staircase.

activities.title: Hackesche Höfe courtyard maze
activities.description: Eight interlinked Art Nouveau courtyards hiding cafés, cinemas, theatres and design studios behind a single Mitte street entrance — easy to lose an afternoon in.
activities.insiderNote: Courtyard IV still has its original 1907 majolica-tiled facade — most visitors walk straight through without looking up.

activities.title: Vietnamese hotpot at Dong Xuan Center
activities.description: A sprawling Vietnamese wholesale market in old GDR industrial halls in Lichtenberg — canteen-style hotpot and pho stalls that most tourists never hear about.
activities.insiderNote: Take tram M8 out to Herzbergstraße; the best hotpot stall is tucked in Hall 9, cash only.

activities.title: Reichstag dome (free, registration required)
activities.description: Norman Foster's glass dome spirals up over the parliament chamber with 360° city views — entirely free, but only accessible with advance online registration.
activities.insiderNote: Book at least a few days ahead on the Bundestag website and bring photo ID matching the booking — walk-ups are turned away.

activities.title: Dance hall night at Clärchens Ballhaus
activities.description: A 1913 ballroom that survived both World Wars and the GDR, still hosting tango, swing and salsa nights under its mirrored chandeliers.
activities.insiderNote: Wednesday and Sunday tango nights draw a mixed crowd of grandparents and 20-somethings — no partner or experience needed to join a beginner round.

activities.title: Boxhagener Platz flea market
activities.description: A Sunday neighbourhood flea market in Friedrichshain with better prices and fewer tourists than Mauerpark — old vinyl, GDR ephemera and vintage bikes.
activities.insiderNote: Go right at opening around 9am for the best finds; by midday the good stalls are picked clean.

activities.title: Currywurst at Curry 36
activities.description: Berlin's most argued-about currywurst stand, open since 1980 near Mehringdamm — steaming sausage sliced and doused in curry-spiced ketchup, eaten standing up.
activities.insiderNote: Order it 'mit Darm' (with skin) for the proper Berlin texture — the late-night queue after clubbing is half the experience.

activities.title: Late bar crawl in the Mitte backstreets
activities.description: The unmarked bars around Rosenthaler Platz and Weinbergsweg change names and doors constantly — Kater Blau's industrial riverside setting is the current local favourite.
activities.insiderNote: Follow the crowd of smokers outside an unlabelled door after 11pm; Berlin's best bars famously have no sign.

activities.title: Museum Island day pass
activities.description: One ticket covers the Pergamon, Neues, Altes, Bode and Alte Nationalgalerie museums — the Ishtar Gate and Nefertiti's bust are the headline draws.

activities.title: TV Tower (Fernsehturm) observation deck
activities.description: Ride to 203 metres for the best 360° panorama in the city from the GDR-era Fernsehturm, still Berlin's tallest structure and visible from almost everywhere.
activities.bookingQuery: Fernsehturm TV Tower observation deck ticket Berlin

activities.title: Berlin Wall & Cold War walking tour
activities.description: A guided walk tracing the Wall's path from Checkpoint Charlie to the Topography of Terror, with stories of escape tunnels and the fall of 1989 from a local historian.
activities.bookingQuery: Berlin Wall Cold War walking tour

activities.title: Sachsenhausen Memorial half-day tour
activities.description: A guided visit to the former concentration camp 35 km north of the city — sobering, essential context for understanding Nazi Germany, with transport included.
activities.bookingQuery: Sachsenhausen Memorial half day tour from Berlin

activities.title: Spree river cruise
activities.description: A relaxed boat ride past the Reichstag, Museum Island and the East Side Gallery — an easy way to see Berlin's landmarks strung along the water.

activities.title: Mauermuseum at Checkpoint Charlie
activities.description: The original Cold War escape-tunnel museum at the most famous crossing point, packed with home-made escape vehicles and smuggling contraptions used to flee East Berlin.

activities.title: Craft beer tasting tour
activities.description: Sample Berliner Weisse and small-batch craft beers across three or four Kreuzberg and Friedrichshain breweries with a guide who explains the city's post-Wall brewing revival.

activities.title: Jewish Museum Berlin entry
activities.description: Daniel Libeskind's zigzag titanium building is itself part of the exhibit — voids, tilted floors and the unsettling Garden of Exile trace centuries of Jewish life in Germany.

activities.title: Guided bike tour of hidden Berlin
activities.description: Cover the Wall trail, the Tiergarten and back-street Kreuzberg in one small-group ride, on flat terrain that makes Berlin one of Europe's easiest cities to bike.

activities.title: Cabaret night at Bar Jeder Vernunft
activities.description: A restored 1912 mirrored circus tent hosting chanson, cabaret and variety acts — the closest thing left to 1920s Weimar-era Berlin nightlife.
activities.bookingQuery: Bar Jeder Vernunft cabaret show ticket Berlin
